Title: CsMYB213-CsbHLH35 module regulates theanine biosynthesis in tea plants (Camellia sinensis L.)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the biosynthetic step producing theanine from precursor substrates in tea plants; identified as a key theanine biosynthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutamate-derived and ethylamine-related precursors; exact substrates not specified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point supported by correlation and regulatory targeting, but rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression in leaves changed consistently with theanine content over spring in AJBC and HJC2; highest in root followed by tender stem, old stem and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression in leaves changed consistently with theanine content over spring in AJBC and HJC2; induced by nitrogen fertilizer, organic fertilizer, green manure, and ammonium; highest in root followed by tender stem, old stem and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spring developmental stage; 10 mM or 5 mM ammonium; chemical fertilizer; chemical plus organic fertilizer; green manure intercropping (Chamaecrista rotundifolia, Vicia villosa)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tea leaves; also high theanine in tender stem, root and bud
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to umami and sweet taste of tea and has multiple human health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CsTSI expression is associated with increased theanine accumulation; repression via upstream regulators decreases theanine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTSI expression was tightly and positively correlated with theanine content in tea leaves across spring sampling. 2. CsTSI promoter was directly bound and activated by CsMYB213. 3. CsMYB213-CsbHLH35 co-expression enhanced CsTSI promoter activation more than CsMYB213 alone. 4. Silencing CsMYB213 or CsbHLH35 reduced CsTSI expression, especially under higher ammonium supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome coexpression analysis; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; asODN knockdown of regulators; VIGS of regulators under nitrogen treatments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for increasing leaf theanine through activation by regulatory gene stacking with CsMYB213 and CsbHLH35 and through nitrogen/organic fertilizer management
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of two pivotal theanine biosynthesis genes in leaves; promoter contains MYB-binding cis-elements and G-box motifs, but CsbHLH35 alone did not bind/activate promoter in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CsTSI promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assays

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces the ethylamine precursor for theanine biosynthesis from alanine-related substrate; identified as a key theanine biosynthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Alanine-related substrate; exact substrate not specified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point supported by correlation and regulatory targeting, but rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression in leaves consistently tracked theanine content over spring in AJBC and HJC2; highest in root followed by tender stem, old stem and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression in leaves consistently tracked theanine content over spring in AJBC and HJC2; induced by nitrogen fertilizer, organic fertilizer, green manure, and ammonium; highest in root followed by tender stem, old stem and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spring developmental stage; 10 mM or 5 mM ammonium; chemical fertilizer; chemical plus organic fertilizer; green manure intercropping (Chamaecrista rotundifolia, Vicia villosa)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tea leaves; also high theanine in tender stem, root and bud
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to umami and sweet taste of tea and has multiple human health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CsAlaDC expression is associated with increased theanine accumulation; repression via upstream regulators decreases theanine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsAlaDC expression was tightly and positively correlated with theanine content in tea leaves across spring sampling. 2. CsAlaDC promoter was directly bound and activated by CsMYB213. 3. CsMYB213-CsbHLH35 co-expression enhanced CsAlaDC promoter activation more than CsMYB213 alone. 4. Silencing CsMYB213 or CsbHLH35 reduced CsAlaDC expression, especially under higher ammonium supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome coexpression analysis; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; asODN knockdown of regulators; VIGS of regulators under nitrogen treatments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to enhance theanine by increasing precursor supply, potentially combined with CsTSI and its positive regulators CsMYB213/CsbHLH35
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains MYB-binding cis-elements and G-box motifs; CsbHLH35 alone did not activate promoter in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CsAlaDC promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assays

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Camellia sinensis var. sinensis database source
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with conserved R2 and R3 domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates CsTSI and CsAlaDC promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator of leaf theanine biosynthesis; directly activates two pivotal biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen availability; ammonium treatment; fertilizer regime; green manure intercropping
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Discussion proposes a positive feedback/amplification loop involving CsMYB213 and CsbHLH35, but mechanistic proof is limited
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes theanine accumulation in tea leaves by activating CsTSI and CsAlaDC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly correlated with theanine content in leaves of AJBC and HJC2 during spring; abundant in tender and old stems; induced by ammonium and some fertilizer treatments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly correlated with theanine content in leaves of AJBC and HJC2 during spring; abundant in tender and old stems; rapidly induced after ammonium treatment; highest under chemical fertilizer and elevated under green manure treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spring developmental stage; 10 mM ammonium; 5 mM ammonium; chemical fertilizer; chemical plus organic fertilizer; green manure intercropping
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tea leaves; highest reported theanine in tender stem, root and bud
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to tea umami and sweetness and has human health benefits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis from transcriptomes; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; antisense oligonucleotide knockdown; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; VIGS under different nitrogen levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB213 expression strongly correlated with theanine accumulation and with CsTSI/CsAlaDC expression in tea leaves. 2. CsMYB213 directly bound the CsTSI and CsAlaDC promoters and activated their transcription. 3. Knockdown of CsMYB213 reduced theanine content and decreased CsAlaDC expression, with CsTSI showing a downtrend. 4. CsMYB213 interacted with CsbHLH35, which enhanced its promoter activation activity. 5. CsMYB213 was induced by ammonium and fertilizer treatments associated with increased theanine.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising TF target for breeding or engineering high-theanine tea, especially combined with CsbHLH35 and optimized nitrogen/organic fertilizer management
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologous to OsMYB61 and AtMYB55; OsMYB61 is linked to nitrogen utilization, supporting a nitrogen-responsive role for CsMYB213
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-like transient expression vectors/effectors in tobacco and native CsTSI/CsAlaDC promoters in reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Camellia sinensis var. sinensis database source
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYC-type bHLH containing IPR011598 domain and coiled-coil region
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Co-activation through protein interaction; enhances CsMYB213-mediated transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances CsMYB213 transactivation of CsTSI and CsAlaDC; alone does not activate these promoters in Y1H/DLR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential enhancer partner in a nitrogen-responsive module that strengthens activation of key theanine biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen availability; ammonium treatment; fertilizer regime; green manure intercropping
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Discussion suggests positive feedback/amplification with CsMYB213, but evidence is indirect
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with CsMYB213 in a regulatory complex/module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes theanine accumulation by enhancing CsMYB213-dependent activation of CsTSI and CsAlaDC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly correlated with theanine content in leaves of AJBC and HJC2 during spring; highest in root followed by tender stem, old stem and leaves; induced by ammonium and organic/green manure treatments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly correlated with theanine content in leaves of AJBC and HJC2 during spring; highest in root followed by tender stem, old stem and leaves; rapidly induced after ammonium treatment; elevated under organic fertilizer and green manure treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spring developmental stage; 10 mM ammonium; 5 mM ammonium; chemical fertilizer; chemical plus organic fertilizer; green manure intercropping
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tea leaves; highest reported theanine in tender stem, root and bud
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to tea umami and sweetness and has human health benefits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis from transcriptomes; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; antisense oligonucleotide knockdown; yeast one-hybrid; yeast transcriptional activation assay; dual-luciferase reporter assay; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; VIGS under different nitrogen levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsbHLH35 expression was positively correlated with theanine accumulation and with key biosynthetic gene expression. 2. CsbHLH35 knockdown reduced theanine content in tea leaves. 3. CsbHLH35 alone had no transcriptional activation toward CsTSI/CsAlaDC promoters and did not bind them in Y1H. 4. CsbHLH35 physically interacted with CsMYB213 and strongly enhanced CsMYB213-mediated activation of CsTSI and CsAlaDC. 5. CsbHLH35 was induced by ammonium, organic fertilizer, and green manure treatments associated with higher theanine.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful co-regulator for stacking with CsMYB213 to enhance theanine; may be particularly relevant under nitrogen-management strategies and organic inputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Silencing CsbHLH35 altered CsMYB213 expression differently under 10 N versus 5 N, indicating condition-dependent regulatory interplay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-like transient expression vectors/effectors in tobacco and native CsTSI/CsAlaDC promoters in reporter assays
